heading: Long-term loans to small-business concerns
label: 685 Long-term loans to small-business concerns
notes: References in TextFor definition of “this chapter”, referred to in subsec. (c), see References in Text note set out under .section 661 of this title

children.heading: Authorization
children.label: a Authorization
children.text: Each company is authorized to make loans, in the manner and subject to the conditions described in this section, to incorporated and unincorporated small-business concerns in order to provide such concerns with funds needed for sound financing, growth, modernization, and expansion.

children.heading: Direct loans; loans on participation basis
children.label: b Direct loans; loans on participation basis
children.text: Loans made under this section may be made directly or in cooperation with other lenders, incorporated or unincorporated, through agreements to participate on an immediate or deferred basis.

children.heading: Maximum rate of interest
children.label: c Maximum rate of interest
children.text: The maximum rate of interest for the company’s share of any loan made under this section shall be determined by the Administration: , That the Administration also shall permit those companies which have issued debentures pursuant to this chapter to charge a maximum rate of interest based upon the coupon rate of interest on the outstanding debentures, determined on an annual basis, plus such other expenses of the company as may be approved by the Administration.Provided

children.heading: Maturity
children.label: d Maturity
children.text: Any loan made under this section shall have a maturity not exceeding twenty years.

children.heading: Soundness of loan; security
children.label: e Soundness of loan; security
children.text: Any loan made under this section shall be of such sound value, or so secured, as reasonably to assure repayment.

children.heading: Extension or renewal
children.label: f Extension or renewal
children.text: Any company which has made a loan to a small-business concern under this section is authorized to extend the maturity of or renew such loan for additional periods, not exceeding ten years, if the company finds that such extension or renewal will aid in the orderly liquidation of such loan.
